format_discipline.notes: No frontmatter. First non-empty line is the H1 title. reading.meta.json exists and parses with required fields (word_count, estimated_read_minutes, citations_used, sections). Exactly six ## sections in correct order: Overview, Key Concepts, Worked Example, In Practice, Key Takeaways, References.

citation_discipline.notes: Primary resource [1] (arxiv.org/2404.16957) cited in Key Concepts (Section 2, lines 66, 79, 84), Section 3 (lines 91, 99), Section 4 (lines 105, 111), Key Takeaways (lines 182, 184), and Worked Example. Bibliography lists [1], [2], [3] matching all inline markers. No orphan markers; no orphan bibliography entries.

findings: Minor: opening sentence in Overview is ~35 words ('A post-mortem is a structured written account ... what governance control would have prevented it [2]'). Occasional long sentences present but not pervasive enough to trigger academic_register fail (fewer than 3 occurrences of hedging; active voice used throughout).

Terms defined on first use: 'post-mortem' defined in Overview; 'proximate cause' and 'root cause' defined with plain analogies ('the thing that broke', 'the reason the broken thing was allowed to exist'); 'SRE' spelled out as 'Site Reliability Engineering' on first use (In Practice section). No cold undefined acronyms found.
One idea at a time respected throughout. No jargon dumps with 3+ new terms in a single paragraph.
Concrete-first pattern followed: loan case examples precede abstractions in Sections 1 and 2; triage example grounds Section 3.
No paragraph exceeds 6 sentences; most are 2-4 sentences.
Procedures and sequences rendered as bullet and numbered lists throughout (Include/Avoid lists in Section 1; four-item numbered list in Section 3; Do/Don't bullet lists in In Practice).
Bold-term definitions used consistently: '**Proximate cause**', '**Root cause**', '**post-mortem**', '**No-blame post-mortem**'.

Inline concrete examples present throughout (loan case within Key Concepts; triage case in Worked Example and governance table). Sections introduce concepts with concrete examples rather than deferring to one example section only.
Tables used for comparisons (proximate vs root cause table; root cause to governance fix mapping table).

8 assertions sampled across reading and verified against corpus: post-mortem definition (corpus 5.1), no-blame framing (corpus 5.1/5.6), proximate/root cause distinction and analogy language (corpus 5.3), four root cause categories (corpus 5.3), accountability adequacy test (corpus 5.4), SRE no-blame finding (corpus 7), governance fix specificity criteria (corpus 5.5), governance fix table mapping (corpus 5.5). All 8 assertions trace directly to corpus content. No unsupported assertions found.

verdict: PASS. All checks pass. Format discipline clean — no frontmatter, correct H1, valid sidecar, six sections in order. Word count 1963 is within the 1800–2200 target range; reading time ~9.8 min is within 9–11 min. Primary resource [1] (arXiv 2404.16957) cited in multiple sections with matching bibliography. No forward references — all topic cross-references (10.1–10.6) are legitimate backward references to prior-taught concepts. Readability is strong: terms defined on first use, concrete-first structure, no jargon dumps, SRE acronym spelled out. Formatting meets standards: short paragraphs, lists for sequences, bold-term definitions, inline examples, comparison tables. Scope is on-headline: teaches exactly the four-section post-mortem framework with no scope creep into untaught advanced material. Assessment ID/weight language present in corpus was correctly omitted from the reading artifact. Faithfulness verified across 8 sampled assertions — all trace to corpus.
